description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a warm, medium-brown wood grain finish. The front face is dominated by a convex, glass screen bordered by a metallic trim, with two large, round control knobs symmetrically placed at the lower corners. Between the knobs is a horizontal grille, likely for the speaker, with a series of vertical slats. The sides of the cabinet are plain except for a vented panel on one side, suggesting internal heat dissipation. The back panel is black, perforated for ventilation, and features a protruding, boxy housing for the cathode ray tube, along with several ports and connectors. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century televisions, and the object appears heavy and solidly built.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television with a convex glass screen, metallic trim, and large control knobs, featuring a vented back and side panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, metallic bezel, two large control knobs, and a horizontal speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden panel with a cream-colored vent near the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a black, perforated back panel with a protruding cathode ray tube housing and various ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing an unadorned wooden panel with no visible features.
attribute_bezel_design: Medium-thick metallic bezel with rounded corners, gold or brass finish, and a slight reflective quality.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large round knobs symmetrically placed on the lower front corners, arranged horizontally.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
